cases.eval_name: Reply in a thread with context
cases.output:
The Slack MCP tools (`slack_read_channel`, `slack_send_message`, etc.) aren't available in this environment. The only MCP integration configured is Pencil — there's no Slack MCP server connected.

To use this skill, you'd need to add a Slack MCP server to your Claude Code configuration. Typically that means adding it to `.claude/mcp.json` with your Slack workspace credentials.

Want me to help you set up the Slack MCP integration?

assertions.text: Reads the full thread with slack_read_thread before drafting a reply
assertions.reasoning: The LLM output does not read any Slack thread with slack_read_thread. Instead, it reports that Slack MCP tools are not available in the environment and offers to help set up the integration.

assertions.text: Draft reply references context from the thread (not generic/disconnected)
assertions.reasoning: The LLM output does not contain any draft reply referencing thread context. Instead, it explains that Slack MCP tools are unavailable and offers to help set up the integration. There is no draft reply at all, let alone one that references context from a thread.

assertions.text: Uses slack_send_message with thread_ts parameter to reply in-thread
assertions.reasoning: The LLM output does not use slack_send_message with thread_ts parameter. Instead, it reports that Slack MCP tools are not available in the environment and offers to help set up the integration.

assertions.text: Shows the draft to the user and waits for approval before sending
assertions.reasoning: The LLM output does not show any draft to the user or wait for approval before sending. Instead, it reports that Slack MCP tools are not available and offers to help set up the integration. No draft was composed or presented for review.
